mnemonics.title: 4NF Steps
mnemonics.content: FOR each non-trivial MVD: FIND A→→B, DECOMPOSE into (A∪B) and (R-(B-A))

mnemonics.title: Trivial MVD Check
mnemonics.content: TRIVIAL: B ⊆ A OR A∪B = All Attributes (Complete set)

acronyms.usage: A constraint where one attribute determines a set of values for another attribute independently
Normal form that eliminates multivalued dependencies
One-to-one relationship where each value of A determines exactly one value of B
Generalized dependency where relation can be reconstructed from projections

visualTips.visualization: Draw a table: For FD (StudentID → Name) shows one-to-one. For MVD (StudentID →→ Course) shows one-to-many independent of other columns. Use a grid showing Cartesian product of Course and Hobby values for each Student.
Circle diagram: If B circle is inside A circle OR combined circles cover entire relation = Trivial. If B is partially outside A and doesn't cover all = Non-Trivial.
Flowchart: Identify MVDs → Check if non-trivial → Decompose to two relations → Repeat until no non-trivial MVDs remain → Verify lossless-join

analogies.analogy: Think of a student who can enroll in multiple courses AND have multiple hobbies. These are independent - taking a new course doesn't change their hobbies. This independence causes redundant rows.
Like separating a closet where winter clothes, summer clothes, and accessories are all mixed together. After organizing (decomposition), each type has its own section.

Imagine a music playlist where for each artist, you store their songs AND their tour dates. Adding a new song shouldn't require adding every tour date - but currently it does, creating redundant entries.
